what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG warning-outline icon: an outlined triangle with an exclamation mark inside, using currentColor stroke and no fill.
observations: Root SVG uses viewBox "0 0 24 24" with no explicit width or height.
Styling is stroke-based with fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width="1.5", round line caps, and round joins.
The warning triangle is drawn with one path: "M12 3 L22 20 H2 Z".
The exclamation mark is drawn with one path containing a vertical segment and a small dot-like segment.
standouts: Uses currentColor, so the icon inherits text or parent color.
Single compact SVG contains only two path elements.
The geometry clearly encodes a warning triangle plus exclamation mark.
conventions: Follows common outline-icon SVG conventions: 24px viewBox, no fill, currentColor stroke, rounded caps and joins.
Keeps icon geometry inline in path data rather than referencing external symbols or styles.
Does not include accessibility metadata such as title, role, or aria attributes.
concerns: No embedded title or accessible label is present in this file alone.
The exclamation mark stem and dot are combined in one path, so they cannot be styled independently within the SVG.
notes: A compact currentColor outline warning icon built from two SVG paths.
questions: Should this asset remain purely decorative, or should consuming code provide an accessible label?
Is the 1.5 stroke width intended to be consistent across the icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a warning outline icon, and the SVG content renders an outlined warning symbol.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a reusable warning icon that can adapt to surrounding color and scale through the SVG viewBox.
contribution: Contributes a concise, scalable warning glyph with inherited color and outline styling for systems that need status or alert iconography.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor SVG outline warning icon made of a triangular alert shape and an exclamation mark.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to represent warning or caution states as a reusable stroke-based icon asset.
